# claudeSession

> Session identity and project awareness layer for Claude Code.

claudeSession solves a common pain point for developers running multiple Claude Code sessions simultaneously: every tab shows the same title "Claude Code", making it impossible to tell which terminal belongs to which project.

It hooks into Claude Code's hook system (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, Notification) to track every session's project, status, and last action. A lightweight web dashboard groups sessions by project, shows real-time status (working / waiting / done / crashed), and lets you focus or resume any session with one click.

The tool also detects orphaned Claude processes, supports Windows Terminal tab switching via UI Automation, and displays subagent parent-child relationships as a tree.

Built for developers who routinely have 10–20+ Claude Code sessions open across projects.

## Key pages

- /: Live session dashboard — all projects and sessions with real-time status

## Tech stack

- Backend: Node.js + Express + WebSocket (chokidar for file watching)
- Frontend: React + Vite
- Platform: Windows 11 + Windows Terminal (primary), with graceful fallback for other terminals

## Contact
